title: ranunculus trichophyllus proles lutulentus
intro: Ranunculus trichophyllus proles lutulentus, also known as the lesser celandine or pilewort, is a flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of protoanemonin and other active compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms of urinary tract infections, as well as exhibiting potential antiviral and antifungal properties. In herbal preparations, Ranunculus trichophyllus proles lutulentus is often used in the form of tinctures, infusions, and ointments, although its use should be cautious due to potential toxicity and skin irritation.
